output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 3/3.
Batch 1: Implemented batch 1 / T1. VideoEditorProvider now owns the lightbox state, query, guarded open handler, and MediaLightbox rendering, and the ops type now exposes `setLightboxAssetKey` instead of `registerLightboxHandler`. Verified by diff and search inspection that `onDoubleClickAsset` now returns early when an asset has no `generationId`. Checkpoint file was not written because `.megaplan/plans/lift-the-lightbox-handler-out-20260406-0805/execution_batch_1.json` does not exist in the workspace.
Batch 2: Completed batch 2 / T2. `PreviewPanel.tsx` now delegates asset double-clicks to the provider-owned `onDoubleClickAsset` and no longer owns any lightbox state, query, or rendering. `VideoEditorShell.tsx` has no remaining `CondensedLightboxProvider` or lightbox fallback references; after cleanup it has no net tracked diff against `HEAD`. Checkpoint file was not written because `.megaplan/plans/lift-the-lightbox-handler-out-20260406-0805/execution_batch_2.json` does not exist in the workspace.
Batch 3: Completed batch 3 / T3. `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` now renders under `QueryClientProvider`, and `OverlayEditor.tsx` still accepts `onDoubleClickAsset?: (assetKey: string) => void`. Verification passed: `npx tsc --noEmit` succeeded, searches for `registerLightboxHandler` and `CondensedLightboxProvider` returned zero results, and `MediaLightbox` is absent from `PreviewPanel.tsx` and `VideoEditorShell.tsx` while present in `VideoEditorProvider.tsx` and unrelated features elsewhere in the repo. `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` passed. A temporary reproduction test for the condensed-layout lightbox path also passed and was deleted afterward. Checkpoint file was not written because `.megaplan/plans/lift-the-lightbox-handler-out-20260406-0805/execution_batch_3.json` does not exist in the workspace.

task_updates.executor_notes: Moved lightbox ownership into `VideoEditorProvider`: added provider-level `lightboxAssetKey` state, derived asset/generation lookup, React Query loading via `loadGenerationForLightbox`, cleanup effect, guarded `onDoubleClickAsset`, and provider-level `MediaLightbox` rendering. Updated `TimelineEditorOpsContextValue` to expose `setLightboxAssetKey` in place of `registerLightboxHandler`. Verified by diff inspection and code search that the new open path checks `editor.resolvedConfig?.registry[assetKey]?.generationId` before calling `setLightboxAssetKey`.

Removed the PreviewPanel-local lightbox imports, state, query, effects, and `MediaLightbox` render, and switched `OverlayEditor` to the shared `onDoubleClickAsset` from editor ops. Verified by code search that `CondensedLightboxProvider`, `registerLightboxHandler`, and the old `preview-lightbox` / `condensed-lightbox` query keys are absent from the video-editor component/context/hook surface. Inspected the condensed JSX after wrapper removal and confirmed the `main` subtree, `TimelineEditor`, and `AgentChat` remain correctly nested. `VideoEditorShell.tsx` produced no net tracked diff after cleanup and now matches `HEAD`.

Wrapped `VideoEditorProvider` in `QueryClientProvider` inside `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, matching the provider’s new React Query dependency. Verified `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx` still declares `onDoubleClickAsset?: (assetKey: string) => void` and required no edits. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Searches for `registerLightboxHandler` and `CondensedLightboxProvider` returned zero results across the repo. A targeted `MediaLightbox` search confirmed it is no longer present in `PreviewPanel.tsx` or `VideoEditorShell.tsx` and remains in `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`; additional matches exist in unrelated features outside this change surface. `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` passed. A temporary reproduction test mounted `VideoEditorProvider` without `PreviewPanel`, triggered `onDoubleClickAsset`, observed the mocked `MediaLightbox` open, then the temp file was deleted.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `onDoubleClickAsset` in `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx` now exits unless `editor.resolvedConfig?.registry[assetKey]?.generationId` is present, so assets without generations cannot open the lightbox.

Yes. After removing the wrapper, the condensed branch still renders the same `<main>` subtree directly under the conditional. `TimelineEditor` and `AgentChat` remain inside that branch with correct nesting, and search confirmed there are no remaining `CondensedLightboxProvider` references.
Yes. `rg -n "registerLightboxHandler" .` returned zero results, confirming the old handler-registration pattern is fully removed from the codebase.
